posts.title: New AI Research Paper
posts.text: New AI Research PaperThis is a new AI research paper published recently. AbstractThis paper presents novel findings in the field of artificial intelligence, focusing on deep learning architectures for natural language processing. IntroductionArtificial intelligence has made significant progress in recent yearsвҖҰ MethodologyWe used transformer-based models for our experimentsвҖҰ ResultsOur model achieved state-of-the-art performance on benchmark datasets. ConclusionThis research contributes to the advancement of AI technology.

posts.title: Universal Adversarial Purification with DDIM Metric Loss for Stable Diffusion-AAAI-4.22
posts.text: PaperдҝЎжҒҜ йўҳзӣ®: Universal Adversarial Purification with DDIM Metric Loss for Stable Diffusion жңҹеҲҠеҗҚ+ж—¶й—ҙ: The Fortieth AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AAAI-4.22), 2026е№ҙ дҪңиҖ…: Li Zheng, Liangbin Xie, Jiantao Zhou, He YiMin и®әж–Үзҡ„еҲӣж–°зӮ№йҰ–ж¬ЎжҸҗеҮәдәҶдёҖз§Қй’ҲеҜ№ Stable Diffusion зҡ„йҖҡз”ЁеҜ№жҠ—еҮҖеҢ–жЎҶжһ¶ (UDAP)пјҢйҖҡиҝҮжңҖе°ҸеҢ–ж–°йў–зҡ„ DDIM Metric Loss е№¶з»“еҗҲеҠЁжҖҒиҝӯд»Јзӯ–з•ҘпјҢй«ҳж•ҲгҖҒж— е·®еҲ«ең°жё…йҷӨдәҶеҗ„зұ»з”ҹжҲҗжЁЎеһӢдё“еұһзҡ„еҜ№жҠ—еҷӘеЈ° гҖӮ зҗҶи®әпјҡж— и®әжҳҜж”»еҮ» UNet иҝҳжҳҜ VAEпјҢеҜ№жҠ—ж ·жң¬жңҖз»ҲдјҡеңЁйҡҗз©әй—ҙеј•е…ҘиҜҜе·®пјҢдё”з”ұдәҺжү©ж•ЈжЁЎеһӢзҡ„ Lipschitz иҝһз»ӯжҖ§пјҢиҜҘиҜҜе·®еңЁ DDIM еҸҚжј”иҝҮзЁӢдёӯдјҡиў«жҳҫи‘—ж”ҫеӨ§ гҖӮ зҺ°иұЎпјҡе®һйӘҢи§ӮеҜҹеҲ°пјҢе№ІеҮҖеӣҫеғҸзҡ„ DDIM йҮҚе»ә $L_2$ иҜҜе·®жһҒе°ҸпјҲеҰӮ 0.004пјүпјҢиҖҢеҗ„зұ»еҜ№жҠ—ж ·жң¬зҡ„йҮҚе»әиҜҜе·®еқҮжҳҫи‘—еҒҸй«ҳпјҲеҰӮ PID дёә 0.669пјүгҖӮеӣ жӯӨпјҢиҜҘиҜҜе·®еҸҜдҪңдёәеҢәеҲҶе’Ңж¶ҲйҷӨеҜ№жҠ—еҷӘеЈ°зҡ„зҗҶжғіжҢҮж Ү гҖӮ дёҖгҖҒ дёәд»Җд№ҲиҜҜе·®дјҡиў«ж”ҫеӨ§пјҹи®әж–Үзҡ„ж ёеҝғжҖқи·ҜжҳҜпјҡеҜ№жҠ—ж ·жң¬з»ҸиҝҮ DDIM еҸҚжј”йҮҚе»әеҗҺпјҢе…¶дёҺеҺҹеӣҫзҡ„и·қзҰ»дјҡиў«жҖҘеү§ж”ҫеӨ§гҖӮи®әж–ҮйҖҡиҝҮд»ҘдёӢе®Ңж•ҙзҡ„ж•°еӯҰжҺЁеҜјиҜҒжҳҺдәҶиҝҷдёҖзӮ№ пјҡ е®ҡд№үеҜ№жҠ—ж ·жң¬дёҺеҷӘеЈ°йў„жөӢеҒҸе·®пјҡ еҒҮи®ҫжңүдёҖеј е№ІеҮҖеӣҫеғҸ $x$пјҢж”»еҮ»иҖ…еҠ е…Ҙеҫ®е°Ҹжү°еҠЁ $\delta$пјҲж»Ўи¶і$||\delta||_p \le \xi$пјүпјҢз”ҹжҲҗеҜ№жҠ—ж ·жң¬$x^{adv} = x + \delta$ гҖӮ дёҖдёӘжҲҗеҠҹзҡ„еҜ№жҠ—ж”»еҮ»пјҢеҝ…з„¶дјҡеҜјиҮҙжү©ж•ЈжЁЎеһӢ $\epsilon_\theta$ еңЁйў„жөӢеҷӘеЈ°ж—¶дә§з”ҹжҳҫи‘—еҒҸе·® $Q$пјҲQ д»ЈиЎЁдәәзңјеҸҜеҜҹи§үзҡ„жҳҺжҳҫи·қзҰ»пјүпјҡ $$||\epsilon_{\theta}(x,t,c) - \epsilon_{\theta}(x^{adv},t,c)|| \ge Q$$ (eq. 1) DDIM жӯЈеҗ‘еҠ еҷӘиҝҮзЁӢзҡ„иҜҜе·®зҙҜз§Ҝпјҡ ж №жҚ® DDIM еҸҚжј”зҡ„е®ҡд№үпјҢж—¶й—ҙжӯҘ $t$ зҡ„зҠ¶жҖҒеҸҜиЎЁзӨәдёә пјҡ $$x_t = \sqrt{\overline{\alpha}_t}x + \sqrt{1-\overline{\alpha}_t}\epsilon$$ жҚ®жӯӨи®Ўз®—е№ІеҮҖж ·жң¬е’ҢеҜ№жҠ—ж ·жң¬еңЁж—¶й—ҙжӯҘ $t$ ж—¶зҡ„и·қзҰ» пјҡ $$||x_t - x_t^{adv}||=||\sqrt{\overline{\alpha}_t}(x-x^{adv})+\sqrt{1-\overline{\alpha}_t}(\epsilon_{\theta}(x,t,c)-\epsilon_{\theta}(x^{adv},t,c))||$$ (eq. 2) д»Је…Ҙе…¬ејҸ 1пјҢеҸҜеҫ—пјҡ$||x_t - x_t^{adv}|| \ge ||\sqrt{\overline{\alpha}_t}\delta + \sqrt{1-\overline{\alpha}_t}Q||$ гҖӮ е…ій”®зӮ№пјҡеҪ“ж—¶й—ҙжӯҘ $t$ и¶Ӣиҝ‘дәҺжңҖеӨ§жӯҘж•° $T$ ж—¶пјҢз”ұе®ҡд№үеҸҜзҹҘ $\overline{\alpha}_t \to 0$ гҖӮеӣ жӯӨе…¬ејҸз®ҖеҢ–дёәпјҡ $$||x_t - x_t^{adv}|| \approx Q$$ (eq. 3) еҲ©з”ЁжқҺжҷ®еёҢиҢЁиҝһз»ӯжҖ§пјҲLipschitz ContinuityпјүжҺЁеҜјжңҖз»Ҳи·қзҰ»пјҡ з”ұ DDIM зҡ„еҸҜйҖҶжҖ§еҸҜзҹҘпјҢж—ўз„¶еҜ№жҠ—ж ·жң¬зҠ¶жҖҒ $x_t^{adv}$ жңҚд»Һй«ҳж–ҜеҲҶеёғпјҢеҝ…з„¶еӯҳеңЁдёҖдёӘеҺҹз©әй—ҙзҡ„ж ·жң¬ $xвҖҷ0$пјҢдҪҝеҫ— $x_t^{adv} = q\theta(xвҖҷ_0, t, c)$ гҖӮ иҝҷж„Ҹе‘ізқҖйҮҚе»әеӣҫеғҸ $\hat{x}_0^{adv} \approx xвҖҷ_0$ гҖӮ (е…¬ејҸ 4) еҒҮи®ҫжЁЎеһӢи®ӯз»ғиүҜеҘҪпјҢж»Ўи¶іжқҺжҷ®еёҢиҢЁеёёж•° $L_t$ зҡ„иҝһз»ӯжҖ§жқЎд»¶ пјҡ $$\frac{||x_t - x_t^{adv}||}{||x_0 - x'_0||} \le L_t$$ (eq. 5) е°Ҷе…¬ејҸ 3 е’Ңе…¬ејҸ 4 д»Је…Ҙе…¬ејҸ 5пјҢе№¶дё”з”ұдәҺжү°еҠЁжһҒе°ҸпјҲ$x^{adv} \approx x$пјүпјҢеҫ—еҲ°пјҡ $$||x^{adv} - \hat{x}^{adv}|| \ge \frac{Q}{L_t}$$ (eq. 6) з»ҲжһҒз»“и®әпјҡеӣ дёәеңЁжү©ж•ЈжЁЎеһӢдёӯпјҢеҪ“ $t \to T$ ж—¶пјҢ$L_t \ll 1$ гҖӮеӣ жӯӨпјҡ $$||x^{adv} - \hat{x}^{adv}|| \geq \frac{Q}{L_t} \gg Q$$ (eq. 7) иҝҷеңЁж•°еӯҰдёҠдёҘи°Ёең°иҜҒжҳҺдәҶпјҡеҜ№жҠ—ж ·жң¬з»ҸиҝҮйҮҚе»әеҗҺпјҢе®ғдёҺеҺҹеӣҫзҡ„и·қзҰ»дјҡиў«ж”ҫеӨ§еҲ°дёҖдёӘиҝңеӨ§дәҺ $Q$ зҡ„еҖј гҖӮиҖҢе№ІеҮҖеӣҫеғҸзҡ„йҮҚе»әи·қзҰ»еҲҷйқһеёёе°Ҹ гҖӮ дәҢгҖҒ з®—жі•еұӮйқўзҡ„е…·дҪ“и®Ўз®—жЎҶжһ¶пјҲInversion OptimizationпјүеҹәдәҺдёҠиҝ°ж•°еӯҰиҜҒжҳҺпјҢUDAP зҡ„е…·дҪ“и®Ўз®—иҝҮзЁӢпјҲд»Јз Ғе®һзҺ°йҖ»иҫ‘пјүеҰӮдёӢ пјҡ 1. еҲқе§ӢеҢ–дёҺжӯЈеҗ‘еҸҚжј”и®Ўз®— (Forward Inversion) зј–з Ғпјҡиҫ“е…ҘеӣҫеғҸ $x$ йҖҡиҝҮеҶ»з»“еҸӮж•°зҡ„ VAE зј–з ҒеҷЁеҫ—еҲ°йҡҗеҸҳйҮҸ $z_0^0 = \mathcal{E}(x)$ гҖӮ жү©ж•Ји®Ўз®—пјҡеңЁз¬¬ $k$ иҪ® EpochпјҢеҲ©з”ЁеҶ»з»“зҡ„ UNet $\epsilon_\theta$пјҢе°ҶйҡҗеҸҳйҮҸ $z_0^k$ йҖҡиҝҮеҮҪж•° $q_\theta$ жү©ж•ЈиҮіжҢҮе®ҡзҡ„ж—¶й—ҙжӯҘ $\hat{T}$пјҲе®һйӘҢдёӯи®ҫе®ҡ $\hat{T}=10$пјүгҖӮи®Ўз®—е…¬ејҸдёәиҝһд№ҳжҰӮзҺҮпјҡ $$q_{\theta}(z_{1:\hat{T}}^{k}|z_{0}^{k}) = \prod_{t=1}^{\hat{T}}q_{\theta}(z_{t}^{k}|z_{t-1}^{k})$$ (eq.8) 2. еҸҚеҗ‘йҷҚеҷӘйҮҚе»әи®Ўз®— (Backward Denoising) йҷҚеҷӘи®Ўз®—пјҡд»ҘеҖ’жҺЁзҡ„йҡҗеҸҳйҮҸ $z_{\hat{T}}^k$ дёәиө·зӮ№пјҲи®ҫдёә $\hat{z}{\hat{T}}^k$пјүпјҢеҲ©з”ЁеҮҪж•° $p\theta$ е°Ҷе…¶йҮҮж ·еӣһж—¶й—ҙжӯҘ 0 гҖӮи®Ўз®—е…¬ејҸдёәпјҡ $$p_{\theta}(\hat{z}_{\hat{T}-1:0}^{k}|\hat{z}_{\hat{T}}^{k}) = \prod_{t=1}^{\hat{T}}p_{\theta}(\hat{z}_{t-1}^{k}|\hat{z}_{t}^{k})$$ (eq. 9) и§Јз ҒпјҡеҲ©з”ЁеҶ»з»“зҡ„ VAE и§Јз ҒеҷЁе°ҶйҮҚе»әйҡҗеҸҳйҮҸиҪ¬жҚўдёәеӣҫеғҸеғҸзҙ пјҡ$\hat{x} = \mathcal{D}(\hat{z}_0^k)$ гҖӮ 3. и®Ўз®—зӣ®ж ҮжҚҹеӨұеҮҪж•°е№¶жӣҙж–°жўҜеәҰ Loss и®Ўз®—пјҡи®Ўз®—еҺҹеӣҫ $x$ дёҺйҮҚе»әеӣҫеғҸ $\hat{x}$ еғҸзҙ зә§еҲ«зҡ„ $L_2$ жҚҹеӨұи·қзҰ»зҡ„е№іж–№ пјҡ $$\mathcal{L}_{DDIM} = ||\mathcal{D}(p_{\theta}(\hat{z}_{\hat{T}-1:0}^{k}|q_{\theta}(z_{1:\hat{T}}^{k}|z_{0}^{k}))) - x||_{2}^{2}$$ (eq.10) жўҜеәҰжӣҙж–°пјҡе®ҡд№үдјҳеҢ–й—®йўҳпјҲP.1пјүпјҢжЁЎеһӢжүҖжңүжқғйҮҚеҶ»з»“пјҢд»…е°ҶйҡҗеҸҳйҮҸ $z_0^k$ и§ҶдёәеҸҜи®ӯз»ғеҸӮж•°гҖӮйҖҡиҝҮеҸҚеҗ‘дј ж’ӯжұӮ $\mathcal{L}_{DDIM}$ еҜ№ $z_0^k$ зҡ„жўҜеәҰпјҢе№¶иҝӣиЎҢдёҖжӯҘдјҳеҢ–жӣҙж–°пјҢеҫ—еҲ° $z_0^{k+1}$ пјҡ $$\min_{z_{0}^{k}}\mathbb{E}_{\epsilon_{\theta},z_{0}^{0}=\mathcal{E}(x)}[\mathcal{L}_{DDIM}(x, z_{0}^{k}, c, \hat{T})]$$ (eq. 11) дёүгҖҒ еҠЁжҖҒйҖҖеҮәжңәеҲ¶зҡ„е®ҡйҮҸи®Ўз®—пјҲDynamic Epochsпјүе№¶дёҚжҳҜжүҖжңүеӣҫзүҮйғҪиҰҒз®—ж»Ў 100 дёӘ EpochпјҢе…·дҪ“жҖҺд№ҲжҺ§еҲ¶еҒңжӯўпјҹ 1. йҳҲеҖј $\tau$ зҡ„дј°з®—ж–№жі• дҪңиҖ…еҲ©з”Ё ImageNet дёӯзҡ„ $N$ еј пјҲ$N=1000$пјүе№ІеҮҖеӣҫеғҸ пјҢи®Ўз®—е®ғд»¬еңЁеҗҢж ·жөҒзЁӢдёӢзҡ„е№іеқҮ $\mathcal{L}_{DDIM}$ дҪңдёәеҹәеҮҶзәҝ пјҡ $$\tau \approx \frac{1}{N}\sum_{n=1}^{N}\mathcal{L}_{DDIM}(x_{n},\epsilon_{\theta},c,\hat{T})$$ (eq.12) е®һйӘҢеҫ—еҮәзҡ„зЎ®еҲҮи®Ўз®—еҖјдёә $\tau = 4 \times 10^{-3}$ гҖӮ 2. еҫӘзҺҜжҺ§еҲ¶еҲӨе®ҡ еңЁеҫ—еҲ°ж–°зҡ„йҡҗеҸҳйҮҸ $z_0^{k+1}$ еҗҺпјҢи®Ўз®—жӯӨж—¶еҜ№еә”зҡ„ $\mathcal{L}_{DDIM}$ гҖӮ Codingи®ҫзҪ® еҲӨе®ҡ 1пјҡиӢҘ $\mathcal{L}_{DDIM} > \tau$ дё”жңӘиҫҫеҲ°жңҖеӨ§ Epochж•° $K$пјҲ$K=100$пјүпјҢеҲҷи®ӨдёәеҜ№жҠ—еҷӘеЈ°жңӘж¶ҲйҷӨе№ІеҮҖпјҢиҝӣе…ҘдёӢдёҖж¬Ўиҝӯд»Ј гҖӮ еҲӨе®ҡ 2пјҡиӢҘ $\mathcal{L}_{DDIM} \le \tau$ пјҢиҜҙжҳҺиҜҘйҡҗеҸҳйҮҸзҡ„йҮҚе»әиЎЁзҺ°е·Із»ҸдёҺеёёи§„е№ІеҮҖеӣҫеғҸж— ејӮпјҢиҫҫеҲ°вҖңе……еҲҶеҮҖеҢ–вҖқзҡ„ж ҮеҮҶ гҖӮеҫӘзҺҜејәеҲ¶еҒңжӯўпјҢе№¶з”Ёи§Јз ҒеҷЁиҫ“еҮәжңҖеҗҺзҡ„еҮҖеҢ–еӣҫеғҸ $xвҖҷ$ гҖӮ ж•°жҚ®йӣҶпјҡVGGFace2 е’Ң CelebA-HQпјҢйҖүеҸ–дәҶ 100 дёӘиә«д»Ҫ (IDs)пјҢжҜҸдёӘ ID еҢ…еҗ« 4 еј еӣҫзүҮ гҖӮ ж”»еҮ»ж–№жі•и®ҫзҪ®пјҡж¶өзӣ– VAE й’ҲеҜ№жҖ§ж”»еҮ» (PID)гҖҒUNet й’ҲеҜ№жҖ§ж”»еҮ» (Anti-DB)гҖҒж··еҗҲж”»еҮ» (MIST)пјҢд»ҘеҸҠеўһејәеһӢиҮӘйҖӮеә”ж”»еҮ» (Anti-DF, MetaCloak) гҖӮ еҸӮж•°дёҺзҺҜеўғпјҡеңЁ 8 еј  NVIDIA A6000 GPU дёҠиҝҗиЎҢпјҢ400 еј  $512\times512$ еӣҫеғҸиҖ—ж—¶зәҰ 20 еҲҶй’ҹпјҲе№іеқҮ 3 з§’/еј пјүгҖӮи®ҫе®ҡжңҖеӨ§ Epoch $K=100$пјҢDDIM еҸҚжј”жҖ»жӯҘж•° $T=20$пјҢжңҖеӨ§ж·ұеәҰ $\hat{T}=10$ гҖӮ иҜ„дј°жҢҮж ҮпјҡдҪҝз”Ё DreamBooth еҫ®и°ғ SD posts.title: вңЁYOLOv8-based Vehicle Detection Android Application-2024
posts.text: YOLOv8 Vehicle Detection Android ApplicationComputer Vision | Mobile AI Deployment | Real-time Object DetectionProject OverviewA prototype system developed in my 2024вҖ”an end-to-end vehicle detection solution ranging from model training to mobile deployment. Based on the YOLOv8 architecture, this project implements real-time vehicle detection on the Android platform. The workflow covers the entire pipeline: dataset preprocessing, model training, format conversion, and Android application development, resulting in a high-performance app with switchable CPU/GPU inference. Core Technology Stack Phase Core Technology Tools / Frameworks Model Training Object Detection, Data Augmentation YOLOv8 / PyTorch / OpenCV Model Conversion Cross-framework Compatibility, Lightweighting ONNX / ncnn Mobile Deployment Real-time Inference, Local Computing Android SDK/NDK / C++ / JNI Engineering Cross-platform Compilation CMake / Gradle Core Pipelineрҹ“Ҡ Data Preparation (VOC/YOLO) вҶ’ рҹҸӢпёҸ Model Training (YOLOv8) вҶ’ рҹ“Ұ Model Conversion (PyTorchвҶ’ONNXвҶ’ncnn) вҶ’ рҹ“ұ Android Deployment Project Highlights High-Performance Inference: Implemented lightweight mobile inference based on the Tencent ncnn framework, supporting one-click switching between CPU and GPU to ensure real-time efficiency. Complete Engineering Pipeline: Covered the full lifecycle from data preprocessing and model training to mobile integration, achieving a closed-loop end-to-end system. Modular Architecture: The Android side utilizes a layered design (JNI + C++ + Java), decoupling the UI from the algorithm logic for better scalability. Real-time Interactive Experience: Features front/rear camera switching and real-time FPS display, compatible with Android 7.0+ devices. Key Features рҹҡ— Real-time Vehicle Detection: High-precision recognition for vehicle categories. вҡЎ High-Performance Inference: Flexible dual-mode switching between CPU and GPU. рҹ“· Dynamic Capture: Seamless switching between front and rear cameras. рҹ“Ҳ Status Monitoring: Real-time FPS monitoring and visualized detection results. posts.title: WiFi7-MLO-4.13
posts.text: Wi-Fi 7 MLOMLO жҳҜд»Җд№ҲвҖ”вҖ”дёҖжқЎйҖҡеҫҖжӣҙеҝ«жӣҙзЁізҡ„вҖңеӨҡиҪҰйҒ“й«ҳйҖҹзҪ‘вҖқMLOпјҲMulti-Link OperationпјүжҳҜ Wi-Fi 7пјҲ802.11beпјүжңҖж ёеҝғжҠҖжңҜпјҢзӣ®зҡ„жҳҜпјҡ жҸҗеҚҮйҖҹеәҰ йҷҚдҪҺж—¶е»¶ еўһејәзЁіе®ҡжҖ§ еҚ•йў‘ж®өжӢҘе өж—¶дёҚж–ӯжөҒ е®ғзҡ„жң¬иҙЁеҸҜд»ҘзҗҶи§Јдёәпјҡ жҠҠеҺҹжң¬вҖңдёҖжқЎи·Ҝиө°еҲ°еә•вҖқзҡ„ Wi-FiпјҢеҸҳжҲҗвҖңеӨҡжқЎи·ҜеҗҢж—¶и·‘гҖҒзҒөжҙ»еҲҮжҚўвҖқзҡ„жҷәиғҪи·ҜзҪ‘гҖӮ ж•ҙдёӘжҠҖжңҜжҢүзЎ¬д»¶иғҪеҠӣеҲҶдёәдёӨеӨ§и·ҜзәҝпјҡеҚ•е°„йў‘и·ҜзәҝгҖҒеӨҡе°„йў‘и·ҜзәҝпјҢжҜҸжқЎи·ҜзәҝеҸҲжңүеҹәзЎҖзүҲгҖҒеўһејәзүҲпјҢе…ұеҗҢжһ„жҲҗе®Ңж•ҙ MLO дҪ“зі»гҖӮ дёҖгҖҒеҚ•е°„йў‘дё–з•Ң вҖ”дҪҺжҲҗжң¬д№ҹиғҪз”ЁдёҠ MLOиҝҷжқЎи·Ҝзәҝйқўеҗ‘жҲҗжң¬ж•Ҹж„ҹгҖҒзЎ¬д»¶з®ҖеҚ•зҡ„и®ҫеӨҮпјҢеҸӘз”Ё 1 дёӘ RadioпјҢе®һзҺ°еӨҡй“ҫи·ҜиғҪеҠӣгҖӮ 1.1 жЁЎејҸжҖ»иЎЁпјҲеҚ•е°„йў‘зұ»пјү жЁЎејҸ е°„йў‘ж•°йҮҸ й“ҫи·Ҝж–№ејҸ з®—жі• жҳҜеҗҰ Wi-Fi 7 ж ҮеҮҶ е®ҡдҪҚ MLSR 1 еҚ•й“ҫи·Ҝе·ҘдҪң еҹәзЎҖ жҳҜ еҹәзЎҖе…Ҙй—Ё eMLSR 1 еҚ•еҸ‘+еҝ«йҖҹеҲҮжҚў жҷәиғҪ еҗҰпјҲеҺӮе•Ҷеўһејәпјү еҚ•е°„йў‘жңҖдјҳж–№жЎҲ 1.2 ж•…дәӢи®Іи§Јв‘  MLSRпјҡMLO зҡ„вҖңеҹәзЎҖе…ҘеңәеҲёвҖқе°ұеғҸдёҖдёӘдәәдёҖж¬ЎеҸӘиғҪиө°дёҖжқЎи·ҜгҖӮ еҸӘжңү 1 дёӘе°„йў‘ еҗҢдёҖж—¶й—ҙеҸӘеңЁдёҖдёӘйў‘ж®өдј иҫ“ еҸӘжҳҜж”ҜжҢҒдәҶ MLO жЎҶжһ¶пјҢжІЎжңүе№¶иЎҢгҖҒжІЎжңүеҠ йҖҹ еӨҡз”ЁдәҺдҪҺз«Ҝ STAгҖҒдҪҺжҲҗжң¬иҠҜзүҮ в‘Ў eMLSRпјҡеҚ•е°„йў‘зҡ„вҖңиҒӘжҳҺеҚҮзә§зүҲвҖқMLSR еӨӘејұпјҢMLMR еӨӘиҙөпјҢдәҺжҳҜеҺӮе•ҶеҸ‘жҳҺдәҶ eMLSRгҖӮ зү©зҗҶиҝҳжҳҜ 1 дёӘ Radio иҷҡжӢҹеҮәеҸҰдёҖжқЎй“ҫи·ҜпјҢжҸҗеүҚжү«жҸҸгҖҒжҸҗеүҚи®ӨиҜҒгҖҒжҸҗеүҚзӣ‘еҗ¬ дё»и·ҜжӢҘе өж—¶пјҢз§’еҲҮеӨҮз”Ёи·ҜпјҢдёҚз”ЁйҮҚж–°жҸЎжүӢ дҪҺжҲҗжң¬е®һзҺ°жҺҘиҝ‘ MLO зҡ„дҪҺж—¶е»¶гҖҒжҠ—жӢҘе ө дёҖеҸҘиҜқзҗҶи§Јпјҡ eMLSR = еҚ•зЎ¬д»¶ + иҪҜиҷҡжӢҹ = дҪҺжҲҗжң¬дјӘеӨҡй“ҫи·Ҝ дәҢгҖҒеӨҡе°„йў‘дё–з•Ң вҖ”вҖ” зңҹжӯЈзҡ„ Wi-Fi 7 жҖ§иғҪзҺӢиҖ…иҝҷжқЎи·Ҝзәҝйқўеҗ‘й«ҳз«Ҝи®ҫеӨҮпјҢз”Ё вүҘ2 дёӘе°„йў‘е®һзҺ°зңҹжӯЈе№¶иЎҢдј иҫ“пјҢжҳҜ MLO зҡ„зҒөйӯӮгҖӮ 2.1 жЁЎејҸжҖ»иЎЁпјҲеӨҡе°„йў‘зұ»пјү жЁЎејҸ е°„йў‘ж•°йҮҸ й“ҫи·Ҝж–№ејҸ з®—жі• жҳҜеҗҰ Wi-Fi 7 ж ҮеҮҶ е®ҡдҪҚ MLMR (STR) вүҘ2 еӨҡй“ҫи·Ҝе№¶иЎҢеҗҢж—¶ж”¶еҸ‘ еҹәзЎҖ жҳҜ ж ҮеҮҶж ёеҝғгҖҒжңҖејәжЁЎејҸ MLMR (NSTR) вүҘ2 еӨҡй“ҫи·ҜеҚ•еҗ‘е№¶иЎҢ еҗҢжӯҘзәҰжқҹ йқһдё»жөҒи®ӨиҜҒ е…је®№иҝҮжёЎ eMLMR вүҘ2 е№¶иЎҢ + жҷәиғҪеҠҹиҖ—дјҳеҢ– жҷәиғҪ еҗҰпјҲеўһејәпјү жңӘжқҘж–№еҗ‘пјҢжҡӮжңӘжҲҗзҶҹ 2.2 ж•…дәӢи®Іи§Јв‘  MLMR (STR)пјҡMLO зҡ„вҖңе®Ңе…ЁдҪ“вҖқзӣёеҪ“дәҺеӨҡиҪҰйҒ“еҗҢж—¶и·‘иҪҰгҖӮ еӨҡдёӘе°„йў‘еҗҢж—¶е·ҘдҪң еӨҡйў‘ж®өеҗҢж—¶еҸ‘гҖҒеҗҢж—¶ж”¶ йҖҹзҺҮеҸ еҠ гҖҒж—¶е»¶жһҒдҪҺгҖҒдёҖжқЎе өдәҶдёҚеҪұе“Қе…¶д»– Wi-Fi 7 зңҹжӯЈзҡ„ж ҮеҮҶж ёеҝғиғҪеҠӣ в‘Ў NSTRпјҡзЎ¬д»¶еҸ—йҷҗзҡ„вҖңжҠҳдёӯж–№жЎҲвҖқ еҸҜд»ҘеӨҡй“ҫи·Ҝе№¶иЎҢпјҢдҪҶеҸӘиғҪеҗҢж—¶еҸ‘ or еҗҢж—¶ж”¶ йңҖиҰҒдёҘж јеҗҢжӯҘ дёҚеұһдәҺдё»жөҒи®ӨиҜҒпјҢд»…зү№ж®ҠзЎ¬д»¶з”Ё в‘ў eMLMRпјҡжӣҙиҒӘжҳҺзҡ„жңӘжқҘзүҲ еңЁ STR еҹәзЎҖдёҠдјҳеҢ–еҠҹиҖ—гҖҒе№Іжү° з©әй—Ій“ҫи·ҜйҷҚеҠҹзҺҮгҖҒзңҒз”өжөҒгҖҒеҮҸе№Іжү° зӣ®еүҚжІЎжңүжҲҗзҶҹе•Ҷз”Ёж–№жЎҲ дёүгҖҒпјҡеә•еұӮеӨ§и„‘ вҖ”вҖ” MLO зҡ„ MAC еұӮжһ¶жһ„MLO иғҪиҝҷд№ҲзҒөжҙ»пјҢйқ зҡ„жҳҜ MAC еұӮиў«жӢҶжҲҗдёӨеұӮпјҢеғҸдёҖдёӘвҖңжҖ»жҢҮжҢҘйғЁ + еӨҡдёӘеҲҶйҳҹвҖқгҖӮ 3.1 з»“жһ„ Upper MACпјҲжҖ»жҢҮжҢҘйғЁпјү з»ҹдёҖи®ӨиҜҒгҖҒз»ҹдёҖи°ғеәҰгҖҒз»ҹдёҖеәҸеҸ·пјҢеҜ№еӨ–еҸӘжҳҫзӨәдёҖдёӘ MAC ең°еқҖпјҢдёҠеұӮе®Ңе…ЁдёҚзҹҘйҒ“жңүеӨҡй“ҫи·ҜгҖӮ Lower MACпјҲеҗ„й“ҫи·ҜеҲҶйҳҹпјү жҜҸжқЎй“ҫи·ҜиҮӘе·ұеӨ„зҗҶж”¶еҸ‘гҖҒACKгҖҒзңҒз”өпјҢдә’дёҚе№Іжү°гҖӮ Non-MLD MAC е…је®№иҖҒи®ҫеӨҮпјҢдҝқиҜҒдёҚзҝ»иҪҰгҖӮ еӣӣгҖҒжҖ»жұҮе…ЁжЁЎејҸ зұ»еҲ« жЁЎејҸ е°„йў‘ж•° е№¶иЎҢиғҪеҠӣ ж ҮеҮҶпјҹ ж ёеҝғзү№зӮ№ е…ёеһӢеңәжҷҜ еҚ•е°„йў‘ MLSR 1 ж— пјҢд»…еҲҮжҚў жҳҜ еҹәзЎҖ MLO дҪҺз«Ҝзү©иҒ”зҪ‘ еҚ•е°„йў‘ eMLSR 1 иҷҡжӢҹзӣ‘еҗ¬+еҝ«йҖҹеҲҮжҚў еҗҰ дҪҺжҲҗжң¬еўһејә дёӯз«ҜжүӢжңә/е®¶з”ЁAP еӨҡе°„йў‘ MLMR(STR) вүҘ2 зңҹжӯЈе№¶иЎҢж”¶еҸ‘ жҳҜ Wi-Fi 7 жңҖејәж ҮеҮҶ ж——иҲ°гҖҒз”өз«һгҖҒдјҒдёҡAP еӨҡе°„йў‘ NSTR вүҘ2 еҚ•еҗ‘е№¶иЎҢ йқһи®ӨиҜҒ зЎ¬д»¶е…је®№иҝҮжёЎ е·Ҙдёҡзү№ж®ҠеңәжҷҜ еӨҡе°„йў‘ eMLMR вүҘ2 е№¶иЎҢ+жҷәиғҪеҠҹиҖ— еҗҰ жңӘжқҘдјҳеҢ– жҡӮжңӘе•Ҷз”Ё дә”гҖҒжҖ»з»“MLO жҳҜ Wi-Fi 7 зҡ„ж ёеҝғпјҢз”ЁвҖңеӨҡй“ҫи·ҜвҖқи§ЈеҶійҖҹеәҰгҖҒж—¶е»¶гҖҒзЁіе®ҡжҖ§ еҚ•е°„йў‘и·Ҝзәҝ MLSRпјҡеҹәзЎҖиғҪз”Ё eMLSRпјҡеҚ•е°„йў‘жҖ§д»·жҜ”д№ӢзҺӢпјҢиҷҡжӢҹй“ҫи·Ҝз§’еҲҮ еӨҡе°„йў‘и·Ҝзәҝ MLMR(STR)пјҡзңҹжӯЈ Wi-Fi 7пјҢе№¶иЎҢеҠ йҖҹпјҢж ҮеҮҶж ёеҝғ NSTRпјҡиҝҮжёЎе…је®№ eMLMRпјҡжңӘжқҘжҷәиғҪдјҳеҢ– MAC жһ¶жһ„ дёҠеұӮз»ҹдёҖи°ғеәҰпјҢдёӢеұӮзӢ¬з«Ӣж”¶еҸ‘пјҢи®©еӨҡй“ҫи·Ҝж— зјқеҚҸеҗҢгҖӮ

\left\lbrack {t, s}\right\rbrack . $$ иҝҷдёҖеҒҮи®ҫеҜјиҮҙEulerжӣҙж–°иҝ‘дјј(еҸҰи§Ғж–№зЁӢ(9.1.7))пјҢе№¶дә§з”ҹеҰӮдёӢзҡ„жӣҙж–°и§„еҲҷ: $$ {\widetilde{\mathbf{x}}}_{t} = \mathcal{E}\left( {s \rightarrow t}\right) {\widetilde{\mathbf{x}}}_{s} + \left( {\frac{1}{2}{\int }_{s}^{t}\frac{{g}^{2}\left( \tau \right) }{{\sigma }_{\tau }}\mathcal{E}\left( {\tau \rightarrow t}\right) \mathrm{d}\tau }\right) {\mathbf{\epsilon }}_{{\phi }^{ \times }}\left( {{\widetilde{\mathbf{x}}}_{s}, s}\right) , \tag{9.2.1} $$ еҜ№дәҺеҲқе§ӢзӮ№${\widetilde{\mathbf{x}}}_{s}$гҖӮжӯӨж—¶пјҢз§ҜеҲҶеҸҳеҫ—и§ЈжһҗеҸҜи§ЈпјҢд»ҺиҖҢеҫ—еҲ°д»ҘдёӢе®һз”Ёдё”й«ҳж•Ҳзҡ„DDIMжӣҙж–°е…¬ејҸ: е‘Ҫйўҳ9.2.1:DDIM = Eulerж–№жі•(жҢҮж•°Euler)ж–№зЁӢ(9.2.1)дёӯзҡ„жӣҙж–°и§„еҲҷпјҢйҖҡиҝҮеҜ№ж–№зЁӢ(9.1.6)дёӯзҡ„жҢҮж•°з§ҜеҲҶеҷЁеҪўејҸеә”з”Ёж¬§жӢүж–№жі•жҺЁеҜјиҖҢеҫ—пјҢеҫ—еҲ°д»ҘдёӢDDIMжӣҙж–°: $$ {\widetilde{\mathbf{x}}}_{t} = \frac{{\alpha }_{t}}{{\alpha }_{s}}{\widetilde{\mathbf{x}}}_{s} - {\alpha }_{t}\left( {\frac{{\sigma }_{s}}{{\alpha }_{s}} - \frac{{\sigma }_{t}}{{\alpha }_{t}}}\right) {\mathbf{\epsilon }}_{{\phi }^{ \times }}\left( {{\widetilde{\mathbf{x}}}_{s}, s}\right) . \tag{9.2.2} $$ е‘Ҫйўҳзҡ„иҜҒжҳҺгҖӮжҲ‘д»¬дҪҝз”Ёж–№зЁӢ (4.4.2) еҚі $$ f(t) = \frac{\alpha'_t}{\alpha_t}, \ g^2(t) = \frac{\text{d}}{\text{d}t} (\sigma_t^2) - 2 \frac{\alpha'_t}{\alpha_t} \sigma_t^2 = 2\sigma_t \sigma'_t - 2 \frac{\alpha'_t}{\alpha_t} \sigma_t^2. $$ з”ұжӯӨпјҢжҲ‘д»¬еҫ—еҲ° $$ \mathcal{E}(s \to t) = e^{\int_s^t f(u) \text{d}u} = e^{\log \alpha_u |_s^t} = \frac{\alpha_t}{\alpha_s}. $$ So, we have $$ \begin{aligned} \int_s^t \frac{g^2(\tau)}{2\sigma_\tau} e^{\int_\tau^t f(u) \text{d}u} \text{d}\tau &= \int_s^t \frac{g^2(\tau)}{2\sigma_\tau} \frac{\alpha_t}{\alpha_\tau} \text{d}\tau \\ &= \alpha_t \int_s^t \frac{1}{2\sigma_\tau \alpha_\tau} \left( \frac{\text{d}\sigma_\tau^2}{\text{d}\tau} - 2 \frac{\text{d} \log \alpha_\tau}{\text{d}\tau} \sigma_\tau^2 \right) \text{d}\tau \\ &= \alpha_t \int_s^t \frac{\text{d}}{\text{d}\tau} \left( \frac{\sigma_\tau}{\alpha_\tau} \right) \text{d}\tau \\ &= -\alpha_t \left( \frac{\sigma_s}{\alpha_s} - \frac{\sigma_t}{\alpha_t} \right). \end{aligned} $$ жӯӨеҜ№еә”е…ізі»жҸӯзӨәдәҶ DDIM еҸҜд»Ҙиў«и§ЈйҮҠдёәеҜ№жҢҮж•°з§ҜеҲҶеҸҳжҚўзҡ„еҚҠзәҝжҖ§ PF-ODE еә”з”Ёзҡ„дёҖйҳ¶ж¬§жӢүж–№жі•гҖӮиҜҰз»ҶжҺЁеҜјдәҶжү©ж•ЈжЁЎеһӢдёӯ DDIM дёҺ ODE жұӮи§ЈеҷЁд№Ӣй—ҙзҡ„иҒ”зі»гҖӮ

posts.title: The Evolution of Intelligent Communications:From Bit-Centric to Token-Oriented Optimization-The Cycle of the World & Me-LM
posts.text: The Evolution of Intelligent Communications: From Bit-Centric to Token-Oriented OptimizationIntroductionFor decades, wireless communication systems have revolved around optimizing physical-layer resources: bits, power, bandwidth, and latency. The core mathematical frameworkвҖ”rooted in ShannonвҖҷs information theoryвҖ”treats data as sequences of binary digits, and the goal is to maximize reliable bit transmission under resource constraints. Today, the rise of large language models (LLMs) and semantic-aware paradigms is rewriting this playbook. In modern intelligent communications, the fundamental unit of optimization is no longer the bit, but the token. This shift is not just a superficial change in terminology; it represents a full-circle evolution of communication, where the carrier of meaning has returned to its roots, now embodied by model-level semantic representations. This article explores how the optimization paradigm is evolving from traditional bit-centric frameworks to token-oriented, attention-driven resource allocation. 1. The Legacy: Bit-Centric Optimization in Classical CommunicationsTraditional communication systems operate on a clear, well-defined mathematical foundation. Every optimization problemвҖ”from channel coding to power control to bandwidth allocationвҖ”centers on physical variables: Transmit power $P$ Bandwidth $B$ Signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) Bit error rate (BER) Latency $T$ These variables are plugged into classic formulas, such as the Shannon capacity formula $C = B \log_2(1 + \text{SNR})$, to define the systemвҖҷs maximum achievable rate. The core idea is straightforward: maximize the number of reliable bits delivered per unit of resource. However, this paradigm has a critical limitation: it treats all bits as equal. A bit representing noise is given the same priority as a bit carrying critical semantic information. In the era of AI-native applicationsвҖ”where the goal is to convey meaning, not just raw dataвҖ”this bit-level equality is inefficient and wasteful. 2. The Revolution: Token-Oriented Optimization in Semantic CommunicationsThe shift to token-oriented optimization redefines the problem entirely. In semantic communication systems powered by LLMs, the transmitted payload is no longer a stream of bits, but a sequence of tokens. These tokens are discrete, model-specific representations of language, each carrying a different weight of semantic importance. The key innovation here is the use of attention mechanisms to quantify token-level significance. The attention score $A_t$ for each token in a sequence measures its contribution to the overall meaning of the message. This score becomes the new core variable in the optimization problem. Instead of treating all bits equally, the system now prioritizes tokens based on their attention weights. 2.1 The New Optimization FrameworkThe problem evolves from a bit-centric resource allocation problem to a token-centric one, which can be formulated as follows: Objective: Maximize the overall semantic transmission efficiency.$\max \quad \mathcal{E} = \frac{\sum_{t=1}^{K} A_t \cdot \mathbb{I}(t)}{P \cdot B}$Subject to: Total transmit power constraint: $P \leq P_{\text{max}}$ Bandwidth constraint: $B \leq B_{\text{max}}$ Latency constraint: $T = N_{\text{token}} \cdot T_s \leq T_{\text{max}}$ Where: $A_t$ = Attention-based importance score for token $t$ $\mathbb{I}(t)$ = Semantic information carried by token $t$ $N_{\text{token}}$ = Number of tokens selected for transmission $T_s$ = Transmission time per token This formulation directly reflects the shift in priorities. The goal is no longer to maximize the raw bit rate, but to maximize the amount of meaning delivered per unit of power and bandwidth. 2.2 The Core Mechanism: Attention-Driven Token SelectionThe practical implementation of this framework relies on a &#34;Top-K Token Selection&#34; mechanism, which operates as follows: Attention Scoring: The model calculates an attention weight for every token in the input sequence. Ranking: Tokens are ranked based on their attention scores. Pruning: Only the top $K$ most semantically important tokens are selected for transmission. The rest are discarded or compressed. Transmission: The selected token indices and their corresponding embeddings are sent over the air. This process ensures that every resource unit (bit, power, time) is spent on the information that matters most, drastically improving efficiency for AI-native workloads. 3. The Full Circle: How Token Optimization Reinvents Classical PrinciplesA striking observation is that the underlying mathematical structure of the optimization problem remains largely unchanged. The objective is still to maximize an efficiency metric under resource constraints. The key difference is the definition of the variables. Aspect Classical Communications Semantic Communications Core Unit Bit Token Key Variable SNR, BER, bit rate Attention score, token rank Objective Maximize reliable bit throughput Maximize semantic information delivered Optimization Logic Resource allocation over bits Resource allocation over tokens This continuity means that decades of research in classical communications are not obsolete. Instead, they are being repurposed. Algorithms originally designed for bit-level power control, adaptive modulation, and error correction are being adapted to work on token sequences. This &#34;evolution, not revolution&#34; allows the field to build on a solid foundation while embracing the new paradigm. ConclusionThe shift from bit-centric to token-oriented optimization is more than a technical upgrade; it is a paradigm shift that brings communication back to its fundamental purpose: conveying meaning. By treating tokens as the new fundamental units of optimization and leveraging attention mechanisms to prioritize information, intelligent communication systems can achieve unprecedented efficiency. Crucially, this evolution does not discard the past. The core mathematical frameworks of resource allocation and optimization remain as relevant as everвҖ”they are simply being applied to a new, more meaningful set of variables. As we move toward an era of AI-native networking, the &#34;bit&#34; will no longer be the sole focus. Instead, the token, guided by the intelligence of large models, will be the driving force behind the next generation of wireless innovation.

OverviewжҷәиғҪе®ҝиҲҚеӨҡжҷәиғҪдҪ“и·Ҝз”ұзі»з»ҹжҳҜдёҖдёӘзәҜиҪҜд»¶жЁЎжӢҹзҡ„жҷәиғҪе®ҝиҲҚз®ЎзҗҶзі»з»ҹпјҢйҮҮз”ЁеӨҡжҷәиғҪдҪ“жһ¶жһ„е®һзҺ°и®ҫеӨҮеҚҸеҗҢжҺ§еҲ¶е’ҢеңәжҷҜиҮӘеҠЁеҢ–гҖӮзі»з»ҹйҖҡиҝҮеӣӣдёӘж ёеҝғжҷәиғҪдҪ“пјҲжҢҮд»Өиҫ“е…ҘгҖҒж„ҹзҹҘгҖҒжү§иЎҢгҖҒи·Ҝз”ұи°ғеәҰпјүеҚҸеҗҢе·ҘдҪңпјҢжЁЎжӢҹзңҹе®һжҷәиғҪе®ҝиҲҚзҺҜеўғдёӯзҡ„и®ҫеӨҮжҺ§еҲ¶е’ҢеңәжҷҜз®ЎзҗҶгҖӮ ж ёеҝғзү№жҖ§ еӨҡжҷәиғҪдҪ“еҚҸеҗҢпјҡеҹәдәҺж¶ҲжҒҜжҖ»зәҝзҡ„жҷәиғҪдҪ“й—ҙйҖҡдҝЎдёҺеҚҸи°ғ еңәжҷҜеҢ–жҺ§еҲ¶пјҡж”ҜжҢҒеӨҡз§Қйў„и®ҫеңәжҷҜжЁЎејҸпјҲзқЎеүҚгҖҒеӯҰд№ гҖҒеЁұд№җзӯүпјү и·Ҝз”ұеҶ—дҪҷжңәеҲ¶пјҡжҷәиғҪи·Ҝз”ұи°ғеәҰдёҺж•…йҡңжҒўеӨҚ зәҜиҪҜд»¶жЁЎжӢҹпјҡж— йңҖзңҹе®һзЎ¬д»¶еҚіеҸҜиҝҗиЎҢжј”зӨә зҺ°д»ЈеҢ–жҠҖжңҜж ҲпјҡеүҚеҗҺз«ҜеҲҶзҰ»пјҢйҮҮз”ЁжңҖж–°жҠҖжңҜжЎҶжһ¶ жҠҖжңҜжһ¶жһ„еҲҶжһҗ1. ж•ҙдҪ“жһ¶жһ„и®ҫи®Ўзі»з»ҹйҮҮз”ЁеүҚеҗҺз«ҜеҲҶзҰ»зҡ„еҫ®жңҚеҠЎжһ¶жһ„пјҢеҲҶдёәдёүеұӮпјҡ 2. жҷәиғҪдҪ“жһ¶жһ„и®ҫи®Ўзі»з»ҹеҢ…еҗ«еӣӣз§Қж ёеҝғжҷәиғҪдҪ“пјҢеҗ„еҸёе…¶иҒҢпјҡ 2.1 жҢҮд»Өиҫ“е…ҘжҷәиғҪдҪ“ (Command Agent) иҒҢиҙЈпјҡжҺҘж”¶з”ЁжҲ·жҢҮд»ӨпјҢи§ЈжһҗеңәжҷҜйңҖжұӮ иҫ“е…Ҙжәҗпјҡз”ЁжҲ·з•Ңйқўж“ҚдҪңгҖҒAPIи°ғз”ЁгҖҒе®ҡж—¶и§ҰеҸ‘еҷЁ иҫ“еҮәпјҡж ҮеҮҶеҢ–д»»еҠЎжҸҸиҝ° зү№жҖ§пјҡж”ҜжҢҒиҮӘз„¶иҜӯиЁҖжҢҮд»Өи§ЈжһҗпјҲеҸҜйҖүLLMйӣҶжҲҗпјү 2.2 и·Ҝз”ұи°ғеәҰжҷәиғҪдҪ“ (Router Agent) иҒҢиҙЈпјҡеҚҸи°ғжҷәиғҪдҪ“й—ҙйҖҡдҝЎпјҢе®һзҺ°ж¶ҲжҒҜи·Ҝз”ұ ж ёеҝғеҠҹиғҪпјҡ ж¶ҲжҒҜиҪ¬еҸ‘дёҺиҙҹиҪҪеқҮиЎЎ и·Ҝз”ұеҶ—дҪҷдёҺе®№й”ҷжңәеҲ¶ жҷәиғҪдҪ“зҠ¶жҖҒзӣ‘жҺ§дёҺеҝғи·іжЈҖжөӢ еӨҮз”Ёи·Ҝз”ұзӯ–з•Ҙ 2.3 ж„ҹзҹҘжҷәиғҪдҪ“ (Sensor Agent) иҒҢиҙЈпјҡжЁЎжӢҹдј ж„ҹеҷЁж•°жҚ®йҮҮйӣҶ posts.title: вңЁPersonal schedule planner template development with Typst-2025
posts.text: On daily work and academic research, a well-organized and aesthetically pleasing schedule planner is the key to efficient time management. Traditional planning tools, however, are often limited by rigid formatting, poor customizability and monotonous visual presentation. To address these pain points, this project presents a personal schedule planner template built with Typst вҖ” a lightweight yet highly customizable planning solution that combines delicate modular design with a rich color system, perfectly suited for academic research, work task management and daily life planning. Typst дёӘдәәж—ҘзЁӢи§„еҲ’жЁЎжқҝејҖеҸ‘пјҲPersonal Planner TemplateпјүйЎ№зӣ®д»Ӣз»ҚеҹәдәҺ Typst зҡ„дёӘдәәж—ҘзЁӢи§„еҲ’жЁЎжқҝпјҢйҖӮз”ЁдәҺз”ҹжҲҗе№ҙеәҰ/жңҲеәҰи®ЎеҲ’ж–ҮжЎЈгҖӮе®ғйҖҡиҝҮзҒөжҙ»зҡ„й…ҚиүІзі»з»ҹгҖҒзҫҺи§Ӯзҡ„жҜҸж—Ҙи®°еҪ•жЎҶдёҺзҙ§еҮ‘зҡ„еӨҡж ҸеёғеұҖпјҢи®©ж—ҘзЁӢеҶ…е®№жё…жҷ°жҳ“иҜ»пјҢеҗҢж—¶е…јйЎҫи§Ҷи§үзҫҺж„ҹдёҺе®һз”ЁжҖ§гҖӮ дё»иҰҒеҠҹиғҪ й…ҚиүІж–№жЎҲ еҶ…зҪ®8з§Қжҹ”е’Ңй…ҚиүІпјҲзҙ«иүІгҖҒжқҸиүІгҖҒи“қиүІгҖҒзІүзәўгҖҒж·ұзҙ«гҖҒйҮ‘й»„гҖҒжөҶжһңиүІгҖҒж·ұзәўпјүпјӣ йўңиүІйҡҸжңҲд»Ҫ/е‘Ёж¬ЎиҮӘеҠЁеҫӘзҺҜпјҢе®һзҺ°дёҚеҗҢж—¶й—ҙеқ—зҡ„и§Ҷи§үеҢәеҲҶгҖӮ жҜҸж—Ҙи®°еҪ•жЎҶ (Day Box) иҮӘеҠЁж №жҚ®жҳҹжңҹеҮ еҢ№й…Қй…ҚиүІпјӣ еҢ…еҗ«гҖҢж—Ҙжңҹ+жҳҹжңҹгҖҚж Үйўҳж ҸдёҺеҶ…е®№еҢәеҹҹпјҢеёҰиҪ»еҫ®йҳҙеҪұжҸҗеҚҮеұӮж¬Ўж„ҹпјӣ ж”ҜжҢҒд»»ж„Ҹ Typst/Markdown еҶ…е®№пјҲеҲ—иЎЁгҖҒд»Јз Ғеқ—гҖҒж•°еӯҰе…¬ејҸзӯүпјүгҖӮ жЁЎжқҝдё»еҮҪж•° (Planner Template) йЎөйқўй…ҚзҪ®пјҡA4жЁӘеҗ‘гҖҒ4ж Ҹзҙ§еҮ‘еёғеұҖпјҢйҖӮй…Қжү“еҚ°/ж•°еӯ—йҳ…иҜ»пјӣ йЎөзңүжЁЎеқ—пјҡжҳҫзӨәдҪңиҖ…гҖҒе№ҙд»ҪгҖҒеҪ“еүҚж—Ҙжңҹпјӣ ж Үйўҳж ·ејҸпјҡ дёҖзә§ж ҮйўҳпјҲжңҲд»ҪпјүпјҡеҪ©иүІеқ—иғҢжҷҜ+зҷҪиүІж–Үеӯ—пјӣ дәҢзә§ж ҮйўҳпјҲе‘Ёж¬ЎпјүпјҡеҪ©иүІж–Үеӯ—+дёӢеҲ’зәҝиЈ…йҘ°пјӣ еӨҡиҜӯиЁҖеӯ—дҪ“ж”ҜжҢҒпјҡй»ҳи®ӨйҖӮй…ҚгҖҢTimes New Roman + жҖқжәҗе®ӢдҪ“гҖҚгҖӮ и®ҫи®Ўзү№зӮ№ зҒөж„ҹжәҗиҮӘ Typst зӨҫеҢәдјҳз§ҖзӨәдҫӢпјҲdvdtypгҖҒboxedsheetпјүпјӣ й…ҚиүІжҹ”е’ҢдёҚеҲәзңјпјҢйҖӮеҗҲй•ҝж—¶й—ҙйҳ…иҜ»пјӣ еёғеұҖзҙ§еҮ‘дҪҶдёҚжӢҘжҢӨпјҢжңҖеӨ§еҢ–йЎөйқўеҲ©з”ЁзҺҮпјӣ ж ·ејҸдёҺеҶ…е®№еҲҶзҰ»пјҢдҫҝдәҺеҗҺз»ӯз»ҙжҠӨе’Ңжү©еұ•гҖӮ дҪҝз”Ёж–№жі•1. еҜје…ҘжЁЎжқҝд»Јз Ғж®ө 1#import "planner.typ": planner, day-block 2. еҗҜз”ЁжЁЎжқҝд»Јз Ғж®ө 1234#show: planner.with( year: "2025", author: "дҪ зҡ„еҗҚеӯ—") 3. ж·»еҠ ж—ҘзЁӢеҶ…е®№д»Јз Ғж®ө 123456789101112// е®ҡд№үжңҲд»ҪпјҲдёҖзә§ж Үйўҳпјү= January// е®ҡд№үе‘Ёж¬ЎпјҲдәҢзә§ж Үйўҳпјү== Week 1 (Jan 1 - Jan 7)// ж·»еҠ жҜҸж—Ҙи®°еҪ•#day-block("Jan 01", "Wednesday")[ *New Year's Day!* - [x] еҲ¶е®ҡе№ҙеәҰзӣ®ж Ү - [ ] з»ҷе®¶дәәжү“з”өиҜқ] дҪҝз”ЁжЎҲдҫӢпјҡ2026е№ҙ1жңҲж—ҘзЁӢи§„еҲ’д»ҘдёӢд»Јз Ғеұ•зӨәдәҶеҰӮдҪ•иҮӘе®ҡд№үз»„д»¶е№¶з”ҹжҲҗдёҖдёӘе®Ңж•ҙзҡ„жңҲеәҰи§„еҲ’йЎөйқўпјҡ д»Јз Ғж®ө 12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940414243444546474849505152535455565758596061626364656667686970717273747576777879#import "planner.typ": *// иҮӘе®ҡд№үеҪ©иүІжҜҸж—Ҙеқ—пјҲж”ҜжҢҒжүӢеҠЁжҢҮе®ҡйўңиүІзҙўеј•пјү#let day-block-colored(date, weekday, color-index, content) = context { let current-color = color-box.at(calc.rem(color-index, color-box.len())) // еӨ–еұӮзӣ’еӯҗпјҲеёҰйҳҙеҪұпјү block( width: 100%, breakable: false, inset: 0pt, radius: 4pt, stroke: none, fill: white, outset: (bottom: 2pt, right: 2pt), { // ж Үйўҳж Ҹпјҡж—Ҙжңҹ+жҳҹжңҹ box( width: 100%, fill: current-color.lighten(40%), inset: (x: 8pt, y: 6pt), radius: (top: 4pt), stroke: (bottom: 1pt + current-color), grid( columns: (1fr, auto), align: (left, right), text(weight: "bold", fill: current-color.darken(20%), date), text(size: 0.8em, fill: current-color.darken(20%), style: "italic", weekday) ) ) // еҶ…е®№еҢәеҹҹ block( width: 100%, inset: 8pt, stroke: (left: 1pt + current-color, right: 1pt + current-color, bottom: 1pt + current-color), radius: (bottom: 4pt), content ) } )}// еҗҜз”ЁжЁЎжқҝ#show: planner.with( year: "2026 Planner", author: "Ming LI")// ================= дёҖжңҲ ================== January// ------ з¬¬дёҖе‘Ё (1жңҲ1ж—Ҙ-1жңҲ7ж—Ҙ) ------== Week 1 (Jan 1 - жҠҖжңҜд»ҺжңҖеҲқи§ЈеҶіеҹәзЎҖзҡ„ж— зәҝдҝЎеҸ·еҶІзӘҒпјҢдёҖжӯҘжӯҘйҖҡиҝҮзү©зҗҶеұӮжӢ“е®ҪиҪҰйҒ“гҖҒMACеұӮдјҳеҢ–и°ғеәҰпјҢжј”еҸҳжҲҗд»ҠеӨ©й«ҳйҖҹгҖҒй«ҳе№¶еҸ‘дё”иҠӮиғҪзҡ„зҺ°д»Јж— зәҝзҪ‘з»ңзі»з»ҹгҖӮ** рҹҢҝ еҲҶдёҖпјҡи®ҫеӨҮз”ҹеӯҳдёҺдәӨжөҒзҡ„ж•…дәӢи®ҫеӨҮеңЁж— зәҝзҪ‘з»ңдёӯжҳҜеҰӮдҪ•жҺҘе…Ҙе’Ңз»ҙжҢҒиҝҗиҪ¬зҡ„гҖӮ йҳІеҶІзӘҒиө·жәҗпјҡ еҖҹйүҙ 1971 е№ҙ ALOHAnet зҡ„жҖқжғіпјҢеҪ“и®ҫеӨҮеҸ‘зҺ°ж— зәҝз”өжіўдёӯзҡ„дҝЎеҸ·вҖңж’һиҪҰвҖқеҗҺпјҢдјҡйҡҸжңәзӯүеҫ…дёҖж®өж—¶й—ҙеҶҚйҮҚеҸ‘пјҢиҝҷжҳҜдҝқиҜҒж— зәҝзҪ‘з»ңдёҚеҙ©жәғзҡ„еҹәзЎҖжі•еҲҷгҖӮ е…ҘзҪ‘дёүйғЁжӣІпјҡ жүӢжңәиҝһдёҠи·Ҝз”ұеҷЁеҝ…йЎ»жҢүйЎәеәҸз»ҸеҺҶпјҡзҪ‘з»ңеҸ‘зҺ°пјҲжүҫеҜ» SSID еҗҚз§°пјү вһЎпёҸ и®ӨиҜҒпјҲж ёеҜ№еҜҶз Ғпјү вһЎпёҸ е…іиҒ”пјҲжӯЈејҸе»әз«ӢзҪ‘з»ңиҝһжҺҘпјүгҖӮ зңҒз”өдёҺдҝқиҒ”з»ңпјҡ дёәдәҶдёҚиҖ—е°Ҫз”өжұ пјҢжүӢжңәдјҡеңЁе”ӨйҶ’зҠ¶жҖҒе’Ңе…ій—ӯжҺҘж”¶жЁЎеқ—зҡ„зһҢзқЎзҠ¶жҖҒ рҹҳҙ д№Ӣй—ҙеҲҮжҚўгҖӮи·Ҝз”ұеҷЁдјҡжҠҠжҡӮеӯҳзҡ„ж•°жҚ®дҝЎжҒҜеҶҷеңЁе‘ЁжңҹжҖ§е№ҝж’ӯзҡ„**дҝЎж Үеё§пјҲBeaconпјү**йҮҢгҖӮжүӢжңәжҢүж—¶йҶ’жқҘжҹҘзңӢиҝҷдёӘвҖңз•ҷиЁҖжқҝвҖқпјҢеҰӮжһңеҸ‘зҺ°жңүиҮӘе·ұзҡ„ж•°жҚ®пјҢе°ұдё»еҠЁеҸ‘йҖҒ PS-POLL её§жҠҠеҢ…иЈ№еҸ–еӣһжқҘпјҢз„¶еҗҺеҶҚеӣһеҺ»з»§з»ӯзқЎи§үгҖӮ рҹҢҝ еҲҶдәҢпјҡж ёеҝғдё“дёҡжңҜиҜӯи§Јжһҗе·ҘзЁӢеёҲд»¬дёәдәҶи®©ж•°жҚ®и·‘еҫ—еҝ«гҖҒдёҚдёўеҢ…пјҢеҸ‘жҳҺдәҶиҝҷдәӣж ёеҝғжңәеҲ¶пјҡ ISM йў‘ж®өпјҡ ж— йЎ»еҗ‘жңәжһ„з”іиҜ·жҺҲжқғеҚіеҸҜе…Қиҙ№дҪҝз”Ёзҡ„йў‘зҺҮпјҲеҰӮжҲ‘д»¬еёёз”Ёзҡ„ 2.4GHzпјүпјҢй—Ёж§ӣдҪҺпјҢдҪҶеӨ§е®¶йғҪз”Ёе°ұе®№жҳ“йҖ жҲҗжӢҘжҢӨе’Ңе№Іжү°гҖӮ дҝЎйҒ“жҚҶз»‘ (Channel Bonding)пјҡ жҠҠзӣёйӮ»иҫғзӘ„зҡ„ж— зәҝвҖңеҚ•иҪҰйҒ“вҖқеҗҲе№¶жҲҗвҖңеҸҢиҪҰйҒ“вҖқ рҹӣЈпёҸпјҢеҚ•дҪҚж—¶й—ҙеҶ…иғҪи·‘зҡ„иҪҰеӨҡдәҶдёҖеҖҚпјҢи®©ж•°жҚ®дј иҫ“йҖҹеәҰзӣҙжҺҘзҝ»еҖҚгҖӮ QAM (жӯЈдәӨжҢҜе№…и°ғеҲ¶)пјҡ жҸҗеҚҮж— зәҝз”өжіўиҪҪжіўзҡ„вҖңиЈ…иҪҪиғҪеҠӣвҖқгҖӮжҜ”еҰӮ 256-QAM иғҪи®©жҜҸдёҖиҫҶж•°жҚ®иҪҰ рҹ“Ұ дёҖж¬ЎжҖ§иҝҗиҪҪ 8 дёӘжҜ”зү№зҡ„дҝЎжҒҜгҖӮ OFDM (жӯЈдәӨйў‘еҲҶеӨҚз”Ё)пјҡ е°Ҷе®Ҫйҳ”зҡ„дҝЎйҒ“еҲ’еҲҶжҲҗеӨҡжқЎдә’дёҚе№Іжү°зҡ„вҖңзӘ„иҪҰйҒ“вҖқпјҲеӯҗиҪҪжіўпјүпјҢи®©еӨҡи·Ҝж•°жҚ®еҸҜд»Ҙе№¶жҺ’иЎҢй©¶пјҢжҸҗй«ҳйў‘и°ұеҲ©з”ЁзҺҮгҖӮ GI (дҝқжҠӨй—ҙйҡ”)пјҡ еңЁеҸ‘йҖҒзҡ„зӣёйӮ»дёӨдёӘдҝЎеҸ·д№Ӣй—ҙйў„з•ҷзҡ„дёҖж®өвҖңе®үе…Ёи·қзҰ»вҖқ рҹҡ§пјҢз”ЁжқҘйҳІжӯўдҝЎеҸ·йҒҮеҲ°еўҷеЈҒеҸҚе°„пјҲеӨҡеҫ„зҺ°иұЎпјүиҖҢеҜјиҮҙзҡ„дҝЎеҸ·вҖңиҝҪе°ҫвҖқе№Іжү°гҖӮ рҹҢҝ еҲҶдёүпјҡIEEE 802.11 дёҺ Wi-Fi ж ҮеҮҶжј”иҝӣи·Ҝзәҝж ҮеҮҶзҡ„еҸ‘еұ•пјҢжң¬иҙЁдёҠжҳҜдёҖйғЁдёҚж–ӯи§ЈеҶівҖңдёҚеӨҹеҝ«вҖқе’ҢвҖңи®ҫеӨҮеӨӘеӨҡдёҚеӨҹз”ЁвҖқзҡ„иҝӣеҢ–еҸІгҖӮ ж—©жңҹжҺўзҙў (802.11 a/b/g)пјҡ зЎ®з«ӢдәҶж— зәҝеұҖеҹҹзҪ‘зҡ„еҹәзЎҖдј иҫ“ж ҮеҮҶпјҢи§ЈеҶідәҶи®ҫеӨҮж— зәҝдёҠзҪ‘д»Һж— еҲ°жңүзҡ„й—®йўҳгҖӮ еӨ§жҸҗйҖҹиҪ¬жҠҳзӮ№пјҡWi-Fi 4 (802.11n)пјҡ йҰ–ж¬Ўеј•е…ҘдәҶ MIMOпјҲеӨҡиҫ“е…ҘеӨҡиҫ“еҮәпјү еӨҡеӨ©зәҝжҠҖжңҜ рҹ“ЎпјҢе№¶й…ҚеҗҲдҝЎйҒ“жҚҶз»‘жҠҖжңҜпјҢе®һзҺ°дәҶзҪ‘йҖҹзҡ„е·ЁеӨ§йЈһи·ғгҖӮ жһҒиҮҙеҚ•жңәйҖҹеәҰпјҡWi-Fi 5 (802.11ac)пјҡ еҚҮзә§иҮіжӣҙй«ҳзә§зҡ„ 256-QAM и°ғеҲ¶ж–№ејҸпјҢиҝӣдёҖжӯҘжҸҗеҚҮдәҶеҚ•еҸ°и®ҫеӨҮеңЁ 5GHz йў‘ж®өдёӢзҡ„дёӢиҪҪжһҒйҖҹгҖӮ й«ҳеҜҶе№¶еҸ‘йқ©е‘ҪпјҡWi-Fi 6 (802.11ax)пјҡ дёәдәҶи§ЈеҶізҺ°д»Је®¶еәӯи®ҫеӨҮиҝҮеӨҡзҡ„й—®йўҳпјҢеј•е…ҘдәҶ OFDMA жҠҖжңҜгҖӮе°ҶеӨ§иҙ§иҪҰеҚ•йҖҒеҸҳжҲҗдәҶвҖңжӢјиҪҰеҸ‘иҙ§вҖқ рҹҡҡпјҢдёҖж¬ЎжҖ§еҗҢж—¶жңҚеҠЎеӨҡеҸ°и®ҫеӨҮпјҢдё“жІ»жҺ’йҳҹжӢҘе өгҖӮ

posts.title: Reading Notes - Deep Learning Book
posts.text: Reading Notes: Deep Learning Book by Ian GoodfellowThis is my reading notes for the classic вҖңDeep LearningвҖқ book by Ian Goodfellow, Yoshua Bengio, and Aaron Courville. Chapter 1: IntroductionKey Concepts Machine Learning: Algorithms that improve automatically through experience Deep Learning: A subset of machine learning using multiple layers Representation Learning: Learning useful representations of data Important Points The curse of dimensionality The importance of feature engineering vs. automatic feature learning Historical context of neural networks Chapter 2: Linear AlgebraMatrix Operations Matrix multiplication properties Eigenvalues and eigenvectors Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) Applications in Deep Learning Weight matrices in neural networks Covariance matrices for data analysis Principal Component Analysis (PCA) Chapter 3: Probability and Information TheoryProbability Distributions Bernoulli, Gaussian, Multinomial distributions Conditional probability and BayesвҖҷ rule Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE) Information Theory Entropy and cross-entropy Kullback-Leibler divergence Mutual information Personal InsightsThis book provides a solid mathematical foundation for understanding deep learning. The explanations are clear, though some sections require careful reading due to the mathematical complexity.
